title: Power BI - REST API Automation For Workspaces, Imports, Reports, Dashboards, And Refresh
applies_to: Programmatic Power BI provisioning and lifecycle management
Automating Power BI workspaces, imports, reports, dashboards, and dataset refresh

confirmed_facts.fact: The Power BI REST API includes workspace operations such as Create Group, Get Groups, Update Group, and workspace user-management operations.
The Imports API can create new content by importing PBIX, JSON, XLSX, and RDL files, and it also supports temporary upload locations for large PBIX files between 1 GB and 10 GB.
The Reports API supports operations such as clone report and export report.
The Dashboards API supports creating, listing, and deleting dashboards, and listing or cloning tiles.

Dataset refresh is triggered through POST /v1.0/myorg/datasets/{datasetId}/refreshes, and for shared capacities a maximum of eight refresh requests per day can be initiated, including scheduled refresh.
Dataset refresh history is available through GET /v1.0/myorg/datasets/{datasetId}/refreshes.

automation_sequence: Identify or create the target workspace through the Groups API.
Import a PBIX, JSON, XLSX, or RDL artifact into the workspace using the Imports API.
Capture the resulting dataset and report identifiers.
Configure connections or gateway bindings if the imported semantic model requires them.
Trigger refresh and monitor refresh history through the Datasets API.
Optionally create or manage dashboards and tiles through the Dashboards API.
Optionally clone reports to new workspaces or bind them to target semantic models when report reuse is needed.
implementation_notes: This is the missing bridge between knowledge of Power BI as a reporting product and actual programmatic artifact management.

For a Microsoft security to Power BI pipeline, the minimum useful automation set is workspace discovery or creation, import or deployment of a report artifact, and dataset refresh plus refresh monitoring.
Use workspace-scoped 'In Group' APIs for automation that targets shared workspaces rather than My workspace.
Refresh quotas and capacity type matter operationally; treat refresh design as part of deployment automation, not as a separate concern.
caveats: A fully automated Power BI solution still needs an artifact strategy such as importing PBIX files or creating datasets by another supported path.
Shared-capacity refresh limits can constrain frequent dashboard updates.
API pages are broad operation catalogs; deeper per-endpoint extraction may still be needed when a project targets a specific lifecycle step.
